key_findings: Thesis-type split remains the dominant signal: structural 8/10 (80%) vs geopolitical 0/6 (0%). This gap has been stable across 3 scoring updates (S528, S536, S543).
XLE is most improved position: +0.46%→+2.85% (5.2x gain). Energy sector structural thesis strengthening.
USO recovery (+3.06% from S528) after -8.95% crash. Oil thesis volatile but directionally correct.
GLD is worst prediction at -5.35% against +5-12% target. The safe-haven thesis has completely failed.
Portfolio in steady state — no new resolutions, Brier stable, directional accuracy stable. Next event: PRED-0017 resolution Mar 29.

diff: Expected Brier ~0.23: confirmed. Expected XLE/XLU improvement: confirmed. Expected GLD worst: confirmed. Expected PRED-0017 INCORRECT: confirmed. New finding: structural vs geopolitical accuracy gap has persisted 3 scoring cycles — this is not noise.

s543_reconciliation.note: Two concurrent sessions scored this. WTI crude direct price: $91.27 (base $93.55 = -2.44%). USO proxy is different instrument. S536 direction accuracy was reported as 10/17=58.8% but recount yields 9/17=52.9%. The transcription error inflated accuracy by 6pp across 2 updates.
